seoTitle: Add a Calendar-Style Date Picker in Power BI Desktop
metaDescription: Learn how to add a calendar-style date picker slicer in Power BI Desktop so viewers can select date ranges without digging through dropdown menus.
intro: A calendar-style date picker makes date filtering feel natural instead of forcing viewers to scroll through long dropdown lists. This guide covers adding a date picker slicer to Power BI Desktop, customizing it, and applying it to real reporting scenarios like sales trend analysis. It's suited to anyone building interactive dashboards that rely on date filtering.

steps.heading: Review the Power BI interface
steps.body: Locate the visualizations pane where slicers are added, and identify the date field in your data model you want to filter by.

steps.heading: Add the calendar-style slicer
steps.body: Insert a slicer visual, assign it to your date field, and switch its format to the between/calendar style so viewers get a date-range picker instead of a list.

steps.heading: Customize the slicer's appearance
steps.body: Adjust formatting options like font, color, and size so the date picker fits the overall look of your report.

steps.heading: Apply it to a practical use case
steps.body: Connect the slicer to visuals tracking performance over time, letting viewers narrow reports to a specific week, month, or custom range.

steps.heading: Wrap up with best practices
steps.body: Test the slicer across different date ranges to confirm all connected visuals respond correctly before sharing the report.

faq.question: What's the difference between a calendar slicer and a regular date slicer in Power BI?
faq.answer: A calendar-style slicer shows a visual date-range picker with a start and end date, while a regular slicer typically lists dates or relative date options in a dropdown or list.

faq.question: Can I set a default date range for the slicer?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure a default selected range so the report opens showing a relevant time period rather than all available dates.

faq.question: Will the date picker work with relative date filtering too?
faq.answer: Power BI slicers support both calendar-style range selection and relative date filtering, so you can choose whichever fits your reporting need.

faq.question: Does adding a date picker slow down the report?
faq.answer: A date slicer adds minimal overhead on its own, though performance depends more on how many visuals it's connected to and the size of your dataset.
